name: Sergipe (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: the central golden band, recalling Brazilian wealth

description: Five equal horizontal bands of green, white, yellow, white and green, with a blue canton in the upper hoist bearing five white stars in the configuration of the Southern Cross.
history: Adopted on 17 March 1920 by State Decree No. 749; designed by Joao Soares Cardoso, the canton's stars represent the Southern Cross constellation.

features.symbolism: Five horizontal bands of green, white, yellow, white and green, with a blue canton bearing five white stars
Blue canton bearing five white stars in the pattern of the Southern Cross

features.role: five white five-pointed stars in the form of the constellation Crux
features.symbolism: the Southern Cross, with each star representing one of the federal regions of Brazil
